title: hemerocallis crocea
intro: Hemerocallis crocea, also known as the Orange Daylily, is a perennial plant species native to China and Japan, characterized by its vibrant orange flowers and sword-shaped leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Hemerocallis crocea include the treatment of various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, as well as its ability to aid in wound healing and reduce inflammation. In herbal preparations, the plant's rhizomes and leaves are typically used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
